name: Corn (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (sweet corn kernels, yellow, raw; Zea mays convar. saccharata)

components.name: starch
components.evidence: Storage glucan; the single largest carbon/energy reservoir of the kernel. Amylase-liberated glucose is the dominant growth substrate (much higher in mature/dent grain, ~70 g/100g, but the FooDB record is the edible sweet-corn form).

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Most abundant free sugar in raw sweet corn; primary readily-fermentable hexose. Free-sugar order is glucose > fructose > sucrose.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Second free sugar.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Disaccharide (glucose+fructose); declines with kernel maturity as it converts to starch (higher in super-sweet sh2 cultivars).

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Not detected in raw sweet corn (appears on cooking/starch hydrolysis). Listed in FooDB but effectively absent in the raw kernel.

components.name: raffinose
components.source: FooDB FOOD00205; maize seed RFO literature
components.evidence: Raffinose-family oligosaccharide of the germ/embryo; present in trace amounts, not individually quantified in FDC.

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence: Trace free monosaccharide reported below quantitation limit; genuine but minor.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Derived: Ritota/Girolamo maize-grain NMR profiling, Molecules 2024 29:4097 PMC11397060 (mean 16.69 mg/100g dry wt) x 0.24 dry-matter fraction of raw sweet corn (USDA FDC 169998, 76% water)
components.evidence: MOST ABUNDANT organic acid of the maize kernel; entirely MISSING from the FooDB profile. Fresh-weight value is a transparent conversion of the grain dry-weight literature mean; order-of-magnitude reliable.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Derived: PMC11397060 (mean 4.91 mg/100g dry wt) x 0.24 dry-matter fraction (USDA FDC 169998)
components.evidence: Second organic acid; MISSING from FooDB. Fresh-weight estimate from grain DW literature.

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Derived: PMC11397060 (mean 2.20 mg/100g dry wt) x 0.24 dry-matter fraction (USDA FDC 169998)
components.evidence: Minor TCA-cycle organic acid; MISSING from FooDB. Fresh-weight estimate.

components.name: trans-aconitic acid
components.source: FooDB FOOD00205 (uncovered 'Aconitic acid'); maize is a classic trans-aconitate accumulator
components.evidence: Characteristic organic acid of Zea mays tissue; genuine and growth-relevant but not cleanly quantified for the kernel here; included qualitatively.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Zein-rich storage protein; amino acids below are the protein-hydrolysate profile.

components.name: L-glutamate (glutamic acid)
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid; major C/N source.

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: Enriched by leucine-rich zein storage protein.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: FooDB lists 'D-Serine'; the protein-derived form is L-serine.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: First limiting amino acid of maize protein.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Second limiting amino acid of maize.

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.evidence: Largely bound (niacytin) in maize; nutritionally partly unavailable but present.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: Food folate = DFE (no fortification).

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Present per FooDB; not quantified in FDC. Included qualitatively.

components.name: choline
components.evidence: Present (~20 mg/100g range in maize kernel); listed qualitatively, exact FDC value not captured.

components.name: vitamin B12
components.evidence: Absent (plant food); FooDB 'Cyanocobalamin' entry is not real kernel content. Cobalamin auxotrophs need supplementation.

components.name: vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: From provitamin-A carotenoids of yellow corn (white corn ~0).

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Provitamin-A carotenoid.

components.name: lutein + zeaxanthin
components.evidence: Dominant carotenoids giving yellow corn its colour; genuine, far exceeds the carotenes. Non-provitamin-A but defining phytochemical.

components.name: vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: Kernel also carries gamma-tocopherol (predominant tocopherol of corn oil, per FooDB).

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant cation.

components.name: phosphorus (phosphate)
components.evidence: Largely stored as phytate (phytic acid) in the germ; partly bio-unavailable but a real P pool.

components.name: calcium
components.evidence: Very low (maize is a poor Ca source).

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Trace micronutrient (soil-dependent).

components.name: sulfur
components.evidence: Present (methionine/cystine + sulfate); genuine growth S source, not quantified in FDC.

components.name: molybdenum (molybdate)
components.evidence: Genuine trace micronutrient cofactor; present, unquantified.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid of corn oil (germ); the corn kernel is mildly oily (~1.35 g fat/100g).

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Concentrated in the germ; corn-oil fatty-acid profile above.

components.name: dietary fiber (insoluble cell wall / arabinoxylan)
components.evidence: Ferulate-cross-linked arabinoxylan pericarp; structural, poorly fermentable.

components.name: water
components.evidence: Dry-matter fraction 0.24 used for organic-acid fresh-weight conversion.

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic metalloid soil contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Cholesterol
remove.reason: animal sterol, biologically implausible in a plant kernel; FooDB predicted (maize sterols are beta-sitosterol/campesterol, kept as uncovered)

remove.name: Naphthalene
remove.reason: polycyclic-aromatic environmental contaminant, not a native kernel nutrient

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: synthetic human contraceptive steroid; implausible/predicted, not a food nutrient (in uncovered list)

remove.name: Cyanocobalamin
remove.reason: vitamin B12 does not occur in plant kernels; FooDB artifact (kept in components as 0 for modelling clarity)

missing_added: citric acid (~4.0 mg/100g FW) - dominant kernel organic acid, absent from FooDB
malic acid (~1.2 mg/100g FW) - absent from FooDB
succinic acid (~0.53 mg/100g FW) - absent from FooDB
trans-aconitic acid (qualitative) - characteristic maize organic acid
quantitative free-sugar split (glucose 3.43 > fructose 1.94 > sucrose 0.89 g/100g) with starch 5.70 g/100g
full amino-acid profile with amounts (USDA, incl. corrected L-serine for FooDB 'D-Serine')
lutein + zeaxanthin (644 µg/100g) - dominant corn carotenoids
keep_uncovered: trans-Ferulic acid / o-Coumaric acid / p-coumaric acid - signature maize cell-wall hydroxycinnamates (genuine, high in corn bran)
Phytic acid - germ phosphate store / mineral chelator (genuine antinutrient)
Feruloyl-beta-sitosterol, 24-Methylcholesterol ferulate, 24-Methylcholestanol ferulate - genuine corn steryl ferulates (corn-fiber-oil components)
Limonene, beta-Pinene, beta-Ionone, alpha-Ylangene, Geosmin - genuine trace aroma volatiles
Maltodextrin - starch-hydrolysis oligomer (genuine in processed corn)
gamma-Tocopherol - predominant corn-oil tocopherol (no distinct BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169998 (Corn, sweet, yellow, raw; SR Legacy) - full sugar/AA/fatty-acid/vitamin/mineral panel

Ritota et al. / Girolamo 2024, Maize Grain Metabolite Profiling by NMR, Molecules 29(17):4097 doi:10.3390/molecules29174097 (PMC11397060) - grain organic acids citric 16.69, malic 4.91, succinic 2.20 mg/100g dry wt
Peruvian Cabanita maize metabolite study, PMC10011086 - qualitative free-sugar/organic-acid confirmation
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is starch (5.7 g/100g in raw sweet corn; up to ~70 g/100g in mature dent grain) plus free glucose 3.43 > fructose 1.94 > sucrose 0.89 g/100g. Organic acids (citric >> malic > succinic) were entirely missing from the FooDB profile and were added; fresh-weight values are transparent conversions of published grain dry-weight NMR means (x0.24 dry-matter) and are order-of-magnitude reliable rather than exact. Profile is for the edible sweet-corn kernel (FDC 169998); mature field/dent grain would be far higher in starch and lower in free sugars/water. Removed FooDB noise: 5 toxic/trace metals, cholesterol, naphthalene, and a synthetic estrogen (implausible/predicted).
